title: 10 best herbal teas for burns
intro_desc: Herbal teas for burns are a natural remedy that provides relief from pain, inflammation, and scarring associated with burn injuries. These teas work by soothing the skin, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal teas that help with burns include chamomile, peppermint, and calendula tea. Drinking these teas can improve lives by reducing discomfort and promoting quicker recovery, allowing individuals to return to their daily activities sooner. For instance, a patient who suffered from second-degree burns on her arm was able to resume work after just two weeks of consuming chamomile tea, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with burns because of its exceptional soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the leaves contains amino acids, vitamins, and minerals that work together to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with burn injuries. As it cools and calms the skin, aloe tea also promotes faster healing by stimulating blood flow and collagen production, leading to reduced scarring and improved skin elasticity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with burn injuries by suppressing prostaglandins, which are chemicals that promote inflammation.

Soothing gel-like mucilage: The gel-like substance in aloe vera leaves forms a protective barrier on the skin, cooling and soothing burned areas by reducing heat transfer and preventing further irritation or infection.

Wound-healing bioactive compounds: Aloe vera contains various bioactive compounds, such as vitamin E, calcium, and potassium, that promote collagen synthesis, accelerate wound healing, and improve tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Leaves: The gel inside the leaves contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that help soothe and calm burn wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and peel them carefully to extract gel.
Combine 3 tablespoons of the extracted aloe vera gel with 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with burns because it provides immediate relief to damaged skin by soothing inflammation, reducing pain, and promoting healing. The mucilages in marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the burned area, locking in moisture and protecting against further irritation. Additionally, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in the tea help to reduce redness, swelling, and blistering, making it an effective natural remedy for burns of various degrees.
remedy_properties: Soothing and calming: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which help to soothe and calm burned skin by reducing inflammation and promoting a protective barrier against further irritation.
Wound healing: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in marshmallow tea may help to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis and improving tissue elasticity.
Pain relief: Marshmallow root contains analgesic properties that can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with burns, making it a useful adjunctive treatment for burn care.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect burned skin.
Stems: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning or evening for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently with cold filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried althaea officinalis leaves and flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the cooled tea to steep for at least 30 minutes before consumption as a topical compress or oral solution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with burns because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin. The tea's flavonoids, particularly californianin and oenanthin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, preventing infection and promoting faster recovery. As a natural remedy, pot marigold tea can provide relief from burn pain and discomfort, making it an effective alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate pain and promote healing by reducing swelling and redness associated with burns.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from further damage caused by oxidation and promoting wound healing.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to aid in wound healing, including burn wounds, due to its ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve tissue regeneration, and enhance blood vessel formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing burn wounds.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers with no signs of mold or contamination for optimal potency.
Dry the fresh flowers in a single layer at room temperature for seven to ten days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers with one cup of boiling water and steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool to body temperature before applying topically to minor burns and wounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When consumed as a tea, it reduces swelling and soothes the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for minor burns. The calming effects of lavender also help to reduce pain and anxiety associated with burn injuries. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent infection by reducing bacterial growth on the affected area, promoting faster healing and minimizing scarring.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in burn wounds.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with burns, promoting a faster recovery process.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants may help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to skin cells and tissue, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting healthy wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to make medicinal teas because they contain a high concentration of saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in burn wounds.
Leaves: The leaves of the English lavender plant are used to make medicinal teas because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm burned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired number of cups.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Add one cup of boiling water to the teapot or infuser and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and discard the solids, then pour the cooled tea into a clean glass.
Drink the tea slowly while warm, ideally 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin in chamomile tea calms irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with burn wounds. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the tea help to promote collagen production, accelerating the healing process. By drinking chamomile tea, individuals can experience relief from pain, itching, and discomfort caused by burns, promoting a faster and more effective recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation caused by burns, making it easier to manage pain and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in chamomile tea can help neutralize free radicals that can damage healthy skin cells, reducing the risk of further tissue damage and promoting faster recovery from burn injuries.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile tea's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress caused by burns, making it easier for patients to relax and cope with their condition, which can aid in the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help soothe and calm burned skin.
Flowers: High in apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Measure one tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers per cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil and steep the herb mixture for 5-7 minutes in a heat-resistant teapot.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids immediately.
Allow the cooled tea to sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before applying it topically as a compress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease pain. The tea's flavonoids and hypericin compounds have been shown to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis and improving tissue repair. Additionally, the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of St John's wort help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for wound recovery. As a result, consuming St John's wort teas may aid in reducing scarring, promoting faster healing, and alleviating discomfort associated with burns.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with burns by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals generated during the burning process, reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair.
Tannins: Act as astringents, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, as well as providing a protective barrier on the skin's surface to prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help soothe and calm burned skin.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and tannins that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, washing them gently under cold running water.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum herb with 1 cup boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep until the liquid reaches a comfortable drinking temperature, about 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean glass or ceramic container, discarding the solids and herbs.
Drink the cooled hypericum perforatum tea three times daily, or as needed, for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with burns because its unique composition of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds can accelerate wound healing and reduce inflammation. The tea's mucilages soothe the burned area, reducing pain and discomfort, while its antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress caused by the burn. Additionally, comfrey's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis promotes tissue repair and regeneration, leading to a faster recovery from burns.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Helps to promote wound healing and tissue repair by stimulating collagen synthesis, promoting cell growth, and improving skin elasticity.
Saponins: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, reducing inflammation, preventing infection, and promoting a healthy environment for burn wounds to heal.
Tannins: Provides antibacterial and antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms that can infect burn wounds and cause further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a compound that promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, making it effective for treating burns.
Rhyzomes: Contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation caused by burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and flowers and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried comfrey leaves and flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh material with 8 ounces boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly while monitoring skin temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the affected area. The plantain's mucilages also create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, promoting faster healing by preventing bacterial growth and reducing pain. Additionally, plantain tea has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, further aiding in the healing process. By drinking plantain teas, you can accelerate the recovery from burns and reduce scarring.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Plantain tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by burn injuries, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which help alleviate pain and swelling associated with burns, making it a natural remedy for soothing discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: The mucilages and saponins present in plantain tea create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, promoting wound healing by reducing moisture loss, preventing infection, and supporting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and protecting burned skin from infection.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain tannins that help reduce inflammation, promote healing, and prevent infection in burn wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Dry the harvested leaves using air drying or dehydrator on low heat for at least 24 hours.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried plantago major leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine ground plantago major with boiling water in a cup to make tea, steeping for 5-10 minutes before straining.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discard solids and serve warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with burns because its potent antioxidants, flavonoids, and volatile oils work synergistically to reduce inflammation, pain, and discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe damaged tissue, while its antimicrobial agents inhibit bacterial growth, preventing infection. Additionally, yarrow's cicatrizant properties promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scar formation. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective way to manage burns and aid in the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as chamazulene and dicoumarol, help reduce swelling and pain in burned areas by reducing inflammation.
Antiseptic properties: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can infect burns and promote infection.

Wound-healing promotion: Yarrow tea's high concentration of vitamin K, antioxidants, and other bioactive compounds help stimulate collagen synthesis, promoting wound healing and tissue repair in burned areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and reduce inflammation in burn wounds.
Buds: Yarrow buds are rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency and quality.
Rinse the harvested plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried or 2 tablespoons of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in 8 ounces boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding any solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool and rest for 10-15 minutes before using it as a topical application or consuming it internally in recommended dosages.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness, swelling, and pain. The antioxidant compounds present in ginger also help to promote healing by protecting the skin from further damage and preventing infection. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic properties can provide relief from discomfort and itching associated with burn wounds. As a result, incorporating herbal ginger teas into a treatment plan may aid in promoting faster recovery and improving overall wound health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with burn wounds, promoting a faster healing process.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger tea can neutralize free radicals caused by the burn, reducing oxidative stress and tissue damage, and helping to prevent infection.

Wound-healing compounds: Ginger contains compounds like 6-gingerol, which has been shown to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving skin elasticity, promoting faster recovery from burns.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help soothe and reduce pain from burn wounds.
Roots: High in antioxidants, roots aid in promoting healthy wound healing and reducing inflammation associated with burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginger root from a reputable source measuring one teaspoon for every 8 oz water.
Boil fresh filtered water in a tea kettle or pot reaching a rolling boil for three to five minutes.
Add the dried ginger root to the boiling water steeping for seven to nine minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup or teapot discarding the solids immediately.
Serve the tea hot with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and honey to taste allowing the patient to sip slowly as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat burns with herbal teas is by using a soothing blend that reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Calendula tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection, while aloe vera tea cools and hydrates the skin. A combination of these herbs can be made into a cooling compress or applied directly to the burn site after it has cooled. This natural remedy helps alleviate pain and speed up the recovery process.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to burns that are treated with herbal teas are skin irritations, wounds, and minor cuts. Herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm the skin, promoting healing and reducing pain and swelling. Other herbs used for these purposes include comfrey, plantain, and turmeric.
